## Claim Under Test

"The archive of a completed adaptive cycle is now self-contained — issue #653's four findings (A attestation, B sink-journal, C candidate binding, D selection/run-gap durability) are correctly and completely fixed across all editions, regression-free." Scope: the accumulated uncommitted bundle diff n2..n6 on base 7daa7fbaefcd6158ad15095324201ec039f7f019 (verified identical to n7's reviewed tree: 72 files, +2219/−150).

## Disproof Attempt

Executed seven attack families; destructive experiments ran in an rsync snapshot (git-init'd, release-tagged) under the scratchpad, zero repo files touched.

**Attack 1 — planner startup surface drops (AC2).** In the snapshot, stripped `--attest-planner-spawn` from each surface one at a time and ran its validator, with green controls before/after each leg. All 8 legs went RED with the needle-specific error: codex workflow-planner.toml, codex adapt SKILL, gitlab TOML, gitlab SKILL, gitea TOML, gitea SKILL → each edition validator "must include: --attest-planner-spawn"; agents/workflow-planner.md → claude validator same; stripping "## Attestation" from claim.js → claude validator "must include: ## Attestation". First run of legs 7-8 was a FALSE red (snapshot missing the release tag — validator failed on the tag check before reaching the needle); re-proven with the tag present and green controls. Every Codex surface is chain-locked. PROVEN.

**Attack 2 — missing attestation → unqualified clean summary?** testAttestationWarningPersistence re-run PASS (contractor-only dispatch-log → archived summary carries column-0 `claim_planner_attested: missing` + verbatim ATTESTATION WARNING; archived ## Closure carries the fields). R1 pre-seed attack SUCCEEDED in fabricating a clean archived summary — quoted run: R1-OBSERVED-SUMMARY = the fake "attested/attested" block survived verbatim; R1-OBSERVED-CLOSURE = `claim_planner_attested: missing`; stdout receipt = verbatim warning. Fence evaluated sufficient (finding A1). Bundle watch-pr lane: status fields only (finding A2). Code read: persistAttestationToSummary is create-if-absent and runs after the source-missing backstop sets result.dest (claim.js:2315→2609), so the backstop lane also persists.

**Attack 3 — sink journal lifecycle.** testSinkTransactionCleanEndToEnd + testSinkTransactionCrashResume re-run PASS; asserts read in full, non-vacuous: real end-to-end --sink in a real git fixture, stdout receipt all-steps-done, journal_disposed === true, all 4 journal paths absent (live+archive × receipt+fallback), `git ls-files` empty, `git status --porcelain` no residue; crash-resume's pre-terminal abort assertions unchanged (journal survives mid-transaction). Code read: disposeSinkJournals (sink-merge.js:806-824, ENOENT-tolerant, warn-only) called at :1589 strictly after all SINK_STEPS + #484 ancestry guard + teardown + finalReceipt capture. gitlab/gitea sink suites green (divergent hand-mirrors behaviorally exercised). Not empirically run: a post-success re-run of the same sink (journal absent + branch deleted → preflight/branch-resolution refusal by construction; #518 cycle-identity guard pre-existing).

**Attack 4 — candidate binding.** Walkthrough cases (a)-(e) re-run PASS and read in full — non-vacuous: real validator binary against real scratch git repos; the recompute in (b)/(d) is genuine (the --current-code-tree seam appears only in (e) where the seam itself is under test); consumer fixtures have no runnable suite, so a pass can only come from hash equality (#475/AC6). Two novel constructions: ADV(f) untracked NEW relevant file after recording → status=1 reason=final_validation_stale; ADV(g) gitignored write → status=0 result=pass (documented boundary — snapshotWorktree PV:2342-2356 scopes to the landable set, parity with the merge gate). Parser attacked with 10 edge inputs (absent/63-hex/indented/trailing-garbage/uppercase/CRLF/fenced/orderings): every malformed shape fails closed to unbound; an indented line never silently binds; a later malformed line cannot dislodge an earlier valid hash into a bypass (the gate still requires tree equality). Ordering: refusal BEFORE side effects proven by code read (gate claim.js:2249-2286 → refuse 2276-2283 → archiveProjectDir 2287) + testKeepOpenArchiveStamp negative leg (wrong hash → finalize_gate_unverified / final_validation_stale, live folder survives, no archive). cmdFinalize shells the validator with fixed argv — the seam is not injectable through finalize. Self-host arm decision-unchanged: boundCandidateHash stays null there and the key is added only when non-null (diff hunks read; zero citation-field lines in the diff → #648 untouched).

**Attack 5 — gap-sweep vacuity.** test-gap-sweep.js → 55 assertions green. Five scratch constructions against the real CLI: G5 (the exact issue-D shape: strict-grammar entry + sweptClasses:[]) → refuse observed_gap_unseeded with the exact tuple — the vacuous hole is closed; G1 sample-mismatch → refuses (exact-tuple, not class-only); G4 end-to-end seed→scan→check → pass mapped:1 noise:1; G3 "- none" back-compat → pass; G2 grammar-miss free text → vacuous pass (finding A3, bounded/documented).

**Attack 6 — generated-surface wipe / prose loss.** generate-routing-surfaces --check (12 surfaces byte-match), edition-sync --check, validate-script-sync all green — no hand-edit remnants and no drift pending a future silent wipe. Independent grep counts: Selection Evidence Docking exactly 1× in each of 6 next surfaces; Run-Gap Manual Seeding 1× in each of 6 plan-run surfaces; validated_candidate_hash on plan-run ×6 + finalize ×4 + contractor ×4; journal-disposal prose on finalize ×4 + all 6 plan-run surfaces (worded "crash-resume journals" — my first grep needle was wrong, verified present); observed_gap_unseeded on finalize ×4; attest flag on TOML ×3 (1×) + adapt SKILL ×3 (3×: command + parenthetical + bundle block) + planner md; scout docking line on md + TOML trio. Provenance grep across all agent-facing surfaces: CLEAN.

**Attack 7 — regression sweep at the exact reviewed tree.** My own runs, all green: root walkthrough (full), codex walkthrough, gitlab + gitea walkthroughs, gitlab + gitea codex walkthroughs, gitlab + gitea sink suites, all 4 runnable contract validators, gap-sweep (55), route-reachability (369), script-sync, generate --check, edition-sync --check. Byte-identity re-verified: claim.js / sink-merge / gap-sweep / validate-workflow-contracts root↔codex pairs, adaptive-schema ×4, closure-contract ×4, planner-TOML trio — all md5-matched.

**AC walk (issue #653 checkboxes):** AC1 delegated-run attests — mechanism proven (flag mandated on every startup surface, back-fill pre-existing, positive-path test green); a live Codex-runtime run is not executable here. AC2 contract coverage — PROVEN (attack 1). AC3 warning preserved — PROVEN on the finalize lane; residuals A1/A2 fenced. AC4 sink hygiene + crash-resume — PROVEN (attack 3). AC5 stale refusal before side effects — PROVEN (attack 4). AC6 mechanical match without re-run — PROVEN (hash-compare only; no suite exists in fixtures). AC7 selection evidence in archive — mechanism proven incl. archive survival (testSelectionEvidenceDocking); advisory-by-design (absence visible as 'absent', never blocking — matches the issue's proposed contract 4). AC8 observed gap must map — PROVEN (attack 5; grammar boundary noted A3). AC9 self-host + #648 unchanged — PROVEN structurally + legacy cases green. AC10 walkthrough passes — PROVEN (my run). AC11 four chains sequential — recorded green twice upstream (n6 close, n7 review) at this exact tree; NOT independently re-run by me end-to-end (time-boxed); my battery independently re-exercised the walkthrough/validator/sink/sync/gap-sweep layers of all four editions at the identical tree (diff stat byte-matched to n7's review). AC12 docs/CHANGELOG — NOT YET MET (zero #653 CHANGELOG entries): owned by n9-docs, a pending downstream node in the frozen plan; finalize (n10) cannot close without it. Not a defect of the code bundle this gate covers.

**Named attacks not run:** the four full npm chains end-to-end; a live Codex-runtime delegated run (AC1's literal scenario); a post-success sink re-run probe. Each is either double-recorded upstream at this exact tree, environmentally impossible here, or safe by construction as argued above.

## Verdict

NOT-REFUTED (confidence: high) — every constructed failure shape either refused with the intended typed reason before side effects, or landed inside an explicitly designed, fenced, low-severity boundary already dispositioned by the review; no attack produced an unqualified-clean archive, a tracked journal, a stale-tree pass, or a vacuous gate on the issue's own failure shapes.
